methodology: Health categories based on createdAt age and presence of inactive label. Sample: 200 most-recently-updated open issues of 943 total.

active.definition: Created <30 days ago, no inactive label

aging.definition: Created 30–90 days ago, no inactive label

stale.definition: Created >90 days ago, no inactive label

zombie.note: Zombie count overlaps with aging/stale age buckets

inactive_label_analysis.insight: Nearly 1/3 of recently-updated open issues carry the inactive label, suggesting a healthy auto-triage bot is running but many issues re-surface after being marked inactive.

unlabeled_analysis.insight: ~12% of open issues have no labels — these need manual triage. Key unlabeled issues: #15982 (Sub-Agent mechanism), #15947 (auto-updater WAF loop), #15962 (API address bug).

top_categories_needing_attention.note: Largest substantive category. High volume of feature requests and bugs.
Provider compatibility and model capability issues.
Assistant settings, parameter passing, and behavior issues.
MCP integration issues — active area of development.

stale_issues_detail.title: Recruitment - Cherry Studio, Welcoming Fellow Travelers

auto_close_candidates.recommendation: Issues marked inactive that have not received a response in 7+ days should be candidates for closing. Review inactive-labeled issues monthly.

health_score.note: Score is indicative; based on 200-issue sample. High zombie ratio (31.5%) is the primary drag.
